annotations.label: Open set in Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: of the Euclidean n -space R n is open if, for every point x in

annotations.note: Mathlib proves IsOpen ↔ ∀ x ∈ s, ∃ ε > 0, ball x ε ⊆ s for any (pseudo)metric space, of which Euclidean n-space is an instance.

annotations.label: Closed interval [0,1] is not open

anchor.snippet: An example of a subset of R that is not open is the closed interval

annotations.note: No direct lemma stating ¬ IsOpen (Icc 0 1) on ℝ was located in Mathlib, though isClosed_Icc and isOpen_Ioo are available.

annotations.label: Open set in a metric space

anchor.snippet: of a metric space ( M , d ) is called open if, for any point x in U

annotations.note: Metric.isOpen_iff at Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Pseudo/Defs.lean:826 gives exactly the ε-ball characterization of openness in a metric space.

annotations.label: Topology and open set

anchor.snippet: on a set X is a set of subsets of X with the following properties

annotations.note: The TopologicalSpace class (Mathlib/Topology/Defs/Basic.lean:71) packages IsOpen with isOpen_univ, isOpen_inter, isOpen_sUnion axioms.

annotations.label: Topological space

anchor.snippet: The set X together with

annotations.note: TopologicalSpace is the standard Mathlib class for the pair of a type with a topology.

annotations.label: Infinite intersection of open sets need not be open

anchor.snippet: Infinite intersections of open sets need not be open

annotations.note: No formal counterexample (e.g., ⋂ n, Ioo (-1/n) (1/n) = {0} not open) was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Metric space is a topological space

anchor.snippet: A metric space is a topological space, whose topology consists of the collection of all subsets that are unions of open balls

annotations.note: Every PseudoMetricSpace inherits a UniformSpace and hence a TopologicalSpace instance; Metric.isOpen_iff (line 826) confirms the topology is generated by open balls.

annotations.label: Unions and finite intersections of open sets

anchor.snippet: The union of any number of open sets, or infinitely many open sets, is open

annotations.note: isOpen_sUnion and isOpen_iUnion give arbitrary unions, and TopologicalSpace.isOpen_inter / IsOpen.inter give pairwise (hence finite) intersections.

annotations.label: Closed set and clopen set

anchor.snippet: A complement of an open set (relative to the space that the topology is defined on) is called a closed set

annotations.note: IsClosed (defined via IsOpen of the complement) and IsClopen (Mathlib/Topology/Defs/Basic.lean:108) appear together in Defs.Basic.

annotations.label: Subspace topology

anchor.snippet: the set Y can be given its own topology (called the 'subspace topology')

annotations.note: The Subtype instance at Mathlib/Topology/Defs/Induced.lean:76 uses TopologicalSpace.induced (↑), which is exactly the subspace topology.

annotations.label: Rationals in an interval open in Q but not R

anchor.snippet: As a concrete example of this, if U is defined as the set of rational numbers in the interval

annotations.note: No specific lemma about the rationals-in-(0,1) example as a subspace-vs-ambient open set was located.

annotations.label: Interior of a subset

anchor.snippet: the maximum (ordered under inclusion) such open set is called the interior of A

annotations.note: interior (Mathlib/Topology/Defs/Basic.lean:118) is the union of all open subsets, i.e., the largest open subset.

annotations.label: Continuous and open functions

anchor.snippet: between two topological spaces

annotations.note: Continuous (structure at Mathlib/Topology/Defs/Basic.lean:151) and IsOpenMap (line 158) directly formalize both notions.

annotations.label: Open sets on the real line

anchor.snippet: An open set on the real line has the characteristic property that it is a countable union of disjoint open intervals

annotations.note: No lemma stating every open subset of ℝ is a countable disjoint union of open intervals was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Clopen set

anchor.section: Clopen sets and non-open and/or non-closed sets
anchor.snippet: Such subsets are known as clopen sets

annotations.note: IsClopen is defined at Mathlib/Topology/Defs/Basic.lean:108 as IsClosed ∧ IsOpen.

annotations.label: Implications among generalized open sets

anchor.snippet: Every α-open subset is semi-open, semi-preopen, preopen, and b-open

annotations.note: The underlying notions (α-open, semi-open, etc.) are not in Mathlib, so the implications are not either.

annotations.label: Regular open iff preopen and semi-closed

anchor.snippet: a subset is a regular open set if and only if it is preopen and semi-closed

annotations.note: Regular-open, preopen, and semi-closed are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arbitrary unions of preopen sets

anchor.snippet: Arbitrary unions of preopen (resp. α-open, b-open, semi-preopen) sets are once again preopen

annotations.note: Preopen and the related generalized open notions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: α-open sets form a finer topology

anchor.snippet: The set of all α-open subsets of a space

annotations.note: α-open sets are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: θ-closed is not formalized in Mathlib, so this characterization is unavailable.

annotations.label: Totally disconnected characterization via preopen sets

anchor.snippet: is totally disconnected if and only if every regular closed subset is preopen

annotations.note: Regular-closed and preopen are not formalized in Mathlib, so this characterization is unavailable.
